extraction.one_line_summary: CORT NIELSEN Magnus won the sprint after Astana controlled multiple late attacks including Nibali testing form on Jebel Street.
tactical_patterns: Astana controlled despite not having obvious leader, preventing late attacks to deliver Cort Nielsen to sprint finish.
Nibali testing race form on Jebel Street climb rather than committing to breakaway suggests reconnaissance mode, not GC focus.
Paris-Roubaix winner Terpstra attacking late stage suggests cobbled specialists recognizing punchy finish suited their power profiles.

rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: neutral: testing form on climbs without commitment reveals he's treating Oman as training block, not target race
rider_signals.CORT NIELSEN Magnus: positive: Astana's willingness to commit entire team for his sprint shows confidence in his finishing speed
rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: neutral: attacking late rather than sprinting indicates he identified finish as too difficult for pure sprinters
rider_signals.STETINA Peter: negative: attacked in race leader's jersey but was isolated, suggesting Trek-Segafredo not providing GC support
